mnemonics: Scalar (S) is a Single number, Vector (V) is Velocity with direction. Partial (P) is like a snapshot, holding other variables constant.
keyPoints: The gradient (∇f) points in the direction of the steepest ascent of a scalar function.
A partial derivative (∂f/∂x) measures the rate of change of a function with respect to one variable, holding others constant.
The Jacobian matrix is the first-order partial derivative matrix of a vector-valued function.
commonMistakes: Treating partial derivatives like ordinary derivatives and forgetting other variables are held constant.
Confusing the gradient (vector) of a function with its directional derivative (scalar).
Incorrectly calculating higher-order mixed partials (e.g., assuming ∂²f/∂x∂y is always equal to ∂²f/∂y∂x without checking Clairaut's theorem conditions).
